subject: Ecology
title: Habitat Loss and Fragmentation
description: A quick, practical guide to Habitat Loss and Fragmentation.

hook.text: Wild pollinators need more than flowers—they need nesting sites, overwintering spots, and corridors.

buildup.text: Monoculture farming replaces diverse meadows with single-crop deserts for pollinators.

discovery.text: Hedgerows, field margins, and wildflower strips provide crucial habitat within farmed landscapes.

twist.text: A pollinator can starve in a landscape of flowers if all bloom at the same time and nothing follows.

climax.text: Continuous bloom sequences—spring through fall—are essential for healthy pollinator communities.

punchline.text: Flowers alone aren't enough. Pollinators need a home.

quiz.question: Why can monocultures be harmful to pollinators?
options: They lack bloom diversity and nesting habitat
